subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data recording and processing are presented both clearly and precisely, with wellâlabelled tables, graphs, error bars and regression statistics.
Data processing is appropriate and accurate, including averaging, uncertainty estimation and linear regressions directly relevant to the research question.
weaknesses: Uncertainties do not fully account for systematic errors (e.g. microphone calibration, room acoustics) and significantâfigure policy is inconsistent.
Key FFT parameters (window size, overlap, reference pressure) are not fully specified, limiting full reproducibility.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication and processing both sit in the top band (scores of 5), while consideration of uncertainties has notable omissions (score of 4). These strand assessments support a holistic Data Analysis score of 5.

strengths: The research question is precisely defined with clear independent and dependent variables and a wellâsituated musicalâphysics context.
The procedural description is detailed, covering instrument setup, controlled variables, measurement devices (dynamometer, caliper, video verification) and FFT extraction steps.
weaknesses: There is a critical inconsistency in plucking the 6th string in the procedure despite the study focusing on the 1st string.
Minor ambiguities remain in thickness values and external factors (amplifier response, room acoustics) are not addressed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of three strands meet the 5â6 band descriptors: context is specific (6) and data collection is reproducible (5). Methodological considerations are described but omit some factors (band 3â4). On balance, this level of performance warrants a holistic score of 5 for Research Design.

strengths: The conclusion clearly links trends (slopes, RÂ² values) back to the hypothesis, correctly interpreting changes in lower and higher harmonics.
The discussion references relevant theoretical ideas on pick stiffness and energy distribution, demonstrating meaningful scientific context.
weaknesses: There is no quantitative comparison to published models, limiting external validation.
The argument occasionally drifts into narrative description rather than critical justification of the results.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is justified and consistent with analysis (band 5â6, score of 5) and includes a relevant scientific context comparison (band 3â4, score of 4). Overall coherence and support justify a holistic score of 5.

strengths: Specific methodological limitations are identified (laptop microphone quality, human plucking variability, caliper precision) with notes on their effects.
Realistic and relevant improvements are proposed (professional microphone, mechanical plucking arm, higherâprecision caliper).
weaknesses: The relative impact of each limitation on data quality is not explained.
Suggested improvements are not quantified in terms of how much uncertainty or bias would be reduced.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands fall into the 3â4 band: specific weaknesses and realistic improvements are described but lack explanation of relative impact (upper 3â4 descriptors). This aligns with a holistic Evaluation score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student formulates a focused research question that specifies the independent variable (plectrum thickness, five discrete values), the dependent variable (sound-pressure level) and the exact domain over which it is to be measured (first eight harmonics of the high-E string).  The introduction supplies clear background about standing waves, overtones and how pick rigidity influences energy distribution, thereby situating the question in a precise musical-physics context.  Because both the physical system (electric guitar, first string) and the measurable outcome (FFT SPL of stated harmonics) are explicitly defined, the context is specific and appropriate, fully satisfying the 5-6 descriptor.

Uncertainties are provided for pick thickness, SPL (via half-range) and some controlled parameters (force, displacement).  Error bars appear on all graphs and are used when discussing trend reliability.  Nevertheless, systematic sources such as microphone calibration or room acoustics are not quantified, and propagation beyond the mean calculation is limited.  These significant omissions place the work solidly in the 3-4 band; the breadth of what is included merits the upper mark (4).

Methodological considerations go beyond a simple list: the student states why each controlled variable matters and describes the chosen value range for the independent variable, connecting these choices to the research aim.  Repetition (five trials), resolution limits (Â±0.05 mm caliper) and use of mean with range/2 uncertainty show awareness of data sufficiency.  However, some factors (microphone frequency response, amplifier settings) are not addressed and the force/velocity control still depends on human judgement.  These omissions indicate a descriptive rather than fully explained treatment, fitting the upper end of band 3-4 (score 4).

Specific weaknessesâlaptop microphone quality, human plucking variability, caliper precisionâare identified and their effect on data quality is briefly noted (e.g., compromises trend, introduces uncertainty).  The discussion stops short of analysing the relative magnitude of each weakness or how they individually alter the outcome.  This description level matches the 3-4 band but is nearer the lower end, hence a score of 3.

The student provides a step-by-step procedure including instrument set-up, microphone distance, force measurement with a dynamometer, video verification of displacement, five trials per pick, and FFT extraction in Audacity.  Lists of controlled variables with methods of control are extensive.  While there is a minor ambiguity (reference to plucking the 6th string in the procedure despite earlier focus on the 1st), the accumulated detail is sufficient for a competent reader to reproduce the investigation with only trivial clarification.  Hence the strand meets the 5-6 band, but the noted inconsistency warrants the lower end of that band (5).

Data processing involves averaging, uncertainty estimation, and linear regression with RÂ² values for each harmonic segment.  Calculations are shown explicitly with examples, and no computational inconsistencies are evident.  The chosen analyses directly address the research question and are executed correctly.  Minor issues (rounding inconsistencies in tables) prevent a perfect 6, but the processing is otherwise appropriate and accurate, warranting a 5.

Improvements such as using a professional microphone, a mechanical plucking arm, and a more precise caliper are realistic and relevant.  The student explains that these would reduce uncertainty and human error, yet the explanations are brief and do not explore how each suggestion would quantitatively enhance reliability or validity.  This descriptive treatment fits band 3-4, with a score of 3 reflecting the moderate depth.

Raw-data tables include units, uncertainties and clear headings; processed tables show means and propagated uncertainties; graphs are labelled with axes, error bars and regression statistics.  The presentation is consistently organised and unambiguous, demonstrating both clarity and precision.  A few labels (e.g., mis-typed âtrailâ for âtrialâ) are minor cosmetic issues, so a score of 5 within the top band is appropriate.

The conclusion references theoretical ideas on pick stiffness and energy distribution, citing background sources and explaining how thicker picks emphasise lower harmonics.  This constitutes a relevant comparison to accepted scientific context.  However, the explanation remains qualitative and does not quantitatively reconcile observed slopes with published models, limiting the depth of justification.  Therefore, the strand sits comfortably in band 3-4 at the upper score of 4.

The student synthesises all data trends, quoting slopes and RÂ² values, and explicitly links these to the original hypothesis.  The direction changes between the 5th and 6th harmonics are correctly interpreted, and discussion of how results align with expectations is coherent.  While well-supported, the argument occasionally drifts into narrative description rather than critical justification, so the lower mark of the top band (5) is most fitting.

annotations.comment: The title clearly defines independent and dependent variables, as well as the scope and context, aiding reader orientation.
The research question precisely specifies independent and dependent variables and the measurement domain, ensuring clear reproducibility.
Include discussion of how amplifier characteristics and room acoustics influence wave behavior to deepen the theoretical context.
The student notes material and shape factors but does not justify isolating thicknessâhow might these variables interact in the study?
The wave speed equation is incorrectly rendered as an integral; the correct form is $$v=\sqrt{F_t/\mu}$$.
Specify microphone calibration details and reference pressure (\(p_0\)) to clarify the accuracy of SPL measurements in Audacityâs FFT.
Dependent-variable table omits measurement uncertainty for SPLâinclude device precision and calibration error to improve clarity.
There is an inconsistency between the listed thickness values (1.05 mm) and the research question (1.00 mm), which may impede reproducibility.
Control of attack velocity refers to the 6th string, conflicting with the 1st-string focus and causing methodological ambiguity.
Clarify the role and specifications of the âtelephoneâ in the equipment list to avoid ambiguity in video data collection.
The procedure refers to plucking the 6th string rather than the 1st, creating a critical inconsistency in the method description.
Include specific FFT settings (window size, overlap, resolution) to ensure consistency in spectral analysis.
State the reference pressure (\(p_0\)) used in SPL calculations to contextualize negative decibel values.
The header 'Second trail' in the raw data table is a typoâcorrect to 'Second trial' to maintain professional clarity.
Overlay error bars or indicate uncertainty on the FFT spectrum peaks to reflect the measurement variability of each trial.
Calculating uncertainty as half-range omits propagation of instrument error; implement full uncertainty propagation for accuracy.
Discuss statistical versus systematic uncertainties when reporting mean SPL to improve rigor in data analysis.
Uncertainties are rounded inconsistently (e.g., Â±0.95 vs Â±1.2); adopt a consistent significant-figure policy across the table.
Add the regression equations and corresponding $$R^2$$ values directly on Graph 1 to enhance precision in data presentation.
Listing only a range of $R^2$ values without specifying per harmonic reduces clarityâreport each harmonicâs $R^2$ explicitly.
Include error bars on trend lines for the 6thâ8th harmonics to visually represent propagated uncertainties.
The summary clearly distinguishes increasing SPL for lower harmonics and decreasing SPL for higher ones, highlighting key patterns.
Conclusions present slopes effectively but lack quantitative comparison to published models for external validation.
The student critically reflects on large random errors relative to signal strength, demonstrating awareness of data quality.
The method strengths (repeated trials, slow-motion verification) show solid control of random error, enhancing reliability.
Suggested improvements (e.g., mechanical arm) are realistic but lack quantitative explanation of how much uncertainty will be reduced.
The suggestion for further research is broad; outline specific hypotheses and analytical methods for additional harmonics exploration.
Integrate a diagram of node and antinode positions with quantitative spacing to reinforce the theoretical background.
Include axis labels and frequency scales on the harmonics figure to improve clarity of overtones representation.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
